There is also 20% off F\&F clothing, all year long, these various discounts can be extended to a family member with a 2 nd Clubcard available.\* * Life Assurance * Pension Plan\* * Save As You Earn Scheme\* * Paid Family Leave * Bike to Work * Long Service Awards * Commuter Scheme \*Entry requirements and rules of the scheme apply Â **About Us** Our vision at Tesco is to become every customerâ€™s favourite way to shop, whether they are at home or out on the move. Â Our core purpose is â€œServing our customers, communities and planet a little better every dayâ€. Â Serving means more than a transactional relationship with our customers. Â It means acting as a responsible and sustainable business for all stakeholders, for the communities we are part of, and for the planet. Through initiatives like the Tesco Community Fund, our association with Childrenâ€™s Health Foundation, our partnership with FoodCloud and our Stronger Starts programme, we have supported over 21,000 community groups, raised over â‚¬7\.5 million for medical equipment, and provided almost 15 million meals nationwide, to those living in food insecurity. Tesco has operated in the Irish retail market since 1997, and with 170 stores nationwide, we employ over 13,000 people in cities and towns around Ireland, supporting almost 45,000 jobs directly and indirectly. We partner with over 500 Irish suppliers \- of which almost three\-quarters are small and medium enterprises \- which in turn supports almost 13,000 farming families around Ireland. Tesco is the single largest buyer of Irish food and drink in the world, buying â‚¬1\.6 Billion a year; more than any other single country in the European Union, more than even the USA which you perceive as a massive buyer of Irish food. Â We work closely with suppliers across Ireland who are dedicated to making the best products for our customers and this is reflected in the numerous awards our products have won over the years including BlÃ¡s Na hÃ‰ireann, World Steak Challenge and Great Taste Awards. We are proud to have an inclusive culture at Tesco where everyone truly feels able to be themselves. It is embedded in our values: we treat people how they want to be treated. Â At Tesco, we not only celebrate diversity, but recognise the value and opportunity it brings. Â We always want our colleagues to feel they can be themselves at work and we are committed to helping them be at their best. We know life looks a little different for each of us. Thatâ€™s why at Tesco, we always welcome chats about flexible working. Some people are at the start of their careers, some want the freedom to do the things they love. Others are going through life\-changing moments like becoming a carer, nearing retirement, adapting to parenthood, or something else. So, talk to us throughout your application about how we can support. Tesco is committed to celebrating diversity and everyone is welcome at Tesco. We're committed to providing a fully inclusive recruitment process, allowing candidates the opportunity to thrive and inform us of any reasonable adjustments they may require. For further information on the accessibility support we can offer, please click http://roi.tesco\-careers.com/accessibility/ **How can I let Tesco know I need additional support?** Please let us know you need additional support by emailing **tescoireland.recruitment@tesco.ie** This will allow us to understand more about you and how we can best support you through the recruitment process.

title: Customer Service Representative
description: **Position: Customer Service Agent** **Location/Department: Uniphar Group Customer Service** **Reporting to: Customer Service Team Leader** We are currently recruiting for a **Customer Service Agent** within Uniphar Group, Supply Chain and Retail Division. **Objective** We are looking for a highly motivated, professional customer service agent, with a track record in customer support. The candidate should be capable of managing customer calls, order management and successful resolution of customer queries. The successful candidate should be able to provide input and suggestions for improving practices and processes within the team, to enable excellent customer service and satisfaction at all times. **MAIN DUTIES \& RESPONSIBILITIES** * Order processing, including Telephone, Email, Fax Orders, including Reps Orders, Credits and Debits * Advise customers of current Uniphar Group product availability, and order delivery schedules * Resolve product or service queries by logging the customer's query, selecting the best solution type to solve the problem and following up to ensure the resolution is in place and communicated to the customer * Use our CRM tool (BCT) to record, resolve and enhance the customer experience * Assist Customer’s with queries regarding their financial accounts * Highlight any delays in processes or orders to your Team Leader or Manager * Ensure accuracy at all times, minimising the impact of error * Operate at all times in a proactive manner complying with Company set procedures * Achieve Weekly and Monthly KPI’s by delivering agreed targets as set by your Team Leader and Manager * Contribute towards department objectives in line with the overall business strategy * Ad hoc duties as directed by Team Leader and Manager **QUALIFICATION, EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS REQUIRED** * SAP and CRM Experience * Proficient in Microsoft Office Applications * Market and product knowledge is an advantage * Proactive, analytical and pragmatic approach to problem solving * Able to work in a fast\-paced environment * Able to respond flexibly and empathetically to customer needs, managing their expectations effectively * Demonstrate strong interpersonal skills, written and oral communication skills **COMPETENCIES** * An understanding of commercial supply chains an advantage – how products move from Manufacturers to Pre\-wholesalers, to Wholesalers and eventually to end customers and the process steps in between. * A genuine team player, who enjoys helping customers and working with multiple business teams. * Excellent telephone manner and communication skills essential * Excellent time management skills **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process.

title: Data Quality Support Analyst
description: **Line of Service** Internal Firm Services **Industry/Sector** Not Applicable **Specialism** IFS \- Internal Firm Services \- Other **Management Level** Associate **Job Description \& Summary** We are seeking a motivated and detail\-oriented Associate to join our Chief Data Office team. This junior level role provides an excellent opportunity to develop your data skills and contribute to our organisation’s mission of maintaining the highest standards of data governance and quality. You will play a key role in supporting data governance initiatives, ensuring data accuracy, completeness, and consistency across the business. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in the implementation and enforcement of data governance policies, standards, and procedures. * Support data quality monitoring processes, performing data quality checks and identifying anomalies. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to document and maintain data lineage, data definitions, and metadata. * Help maintain data stewardship activities and provide support to data owners and custodians. * Assist in preparing reports and dashboards on data quality metrics for senior management. * Participate in data quality issue identification, root cause analysis, and remediation tracking. * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives related to data governance and quality practices. * Stay updated with industry best practices and regulatory requirements concerning data governance and management. * Provide general administrative support to the Data Governance \& Quality pillar as needed. **Experience \& Qualifications** * Minimum 4 years’ experience in a data management or data qualit y\-focused role. * Third\-level qualification required ; a degree in Information M anagement or related field is desirable. * Good understanding of data governance frameworks, data standard and compliance * Exposure to data management, data governance or data quality concepts * Experience with data catalog and lineage concepts and solutions desirable * Familiarity with data quality frameworks (e.g., DAMA\-DMBOK) desirable. * Experience in quality control, cleansing and remediation. * Proven ability to manage multiple priorities . * Experience working in high\-performing teams and delivering to deadlines . * Foundational project management experience including stakeholder management , task planning, and milestone tracking. **Skills** * Strong attention to detail, analytical mindset and problem\-solving skills * Strong verbal and written communication skills including tailored communications to different stakeholders. * Critical thinking skills, asks clarifying questions, i dentifies gaps and challenges assumptions. * Curious, proactive, and self\-motivated with a strong team ethic. * Organised, with strong prioritisation and time management skills. * Enthusiastic about data quality and its role in driving business value. * Adaptable and resilient in a changing environment. **Unlock your potential with PwC Ireland** W e believe that challenges are better solved together! We’re inspiring and empowering our people to change the world. Powered by the latest technology, you’ll be a part of amazing teams encouraged to collaborate and innovate in a way that creates powerful solutions and makes a positive impact. This purpose\-led work, and our continuous development and encouragement, will help unlock your potential and take your career to the next level. **Enjoy PwC’s perks** We reward your impact, and support your wellbeing, through a competitive compensation package, inclusive employee benefits and flexibility programs that will help you thrive in work and life. Learn more about us at Life@PwC . ( https://www.pwc.ie/careers\-ie/life\-at\-pwc.html ). **Being appreciated for being you** Our most valuable asset is our people and we grow stronger as we learn from one another. We are an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color , national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status . We believe this so much that we have signed up for the Business in the Community Elevate Pledge . ( https://www.bitc.ie/the\-leaders\-group\-on\-sustainability/inclusive\-workplace\-pledge/ ) You can learn more about our culture of belonging and explore our range of inclusive programmes, initiatives, employee resource groups and more at www.pwc.ie . Avoid the confidence gap; you do not have to match all the listed requirements exactly to apply, we’d love to hear from you! We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please email us for more information. **Required Skills** **Optional Skills** Accepting Feedback, Accepting Feedback, Active Listening, Azure DevOps Server, Communication, Database Administration, Database Performance Management, Database Quality Assurance, Database Troubleshooting, Data Error Checking, Data Optimization, Data Profiling, Data Quality, Data Quality Assessment, Data Quality Assurance Testing, Data Quality Automation, Data Science Troubleshooting, Data Validation, Data Warehousing Quality Assurance, Emotional Regulation, Empathy, Inclusion, Intellectual Curiosity, Key Performance Indicators (KPIs), Metadata Management {\+ 5 more} **Desired Languages** *(If blank, desired languages not specified)* **Travel Requirements** Not Specified **Available for Work Visa Sponsorship?** No **Government Clearance Required?** No **Job Posting End Date**

title: Description: HTL-Absolvent Elektrotechnik / Mechatronik (w/m/d) - Montageelektrik Kranmontage
description: * Job Overview * Back to Career Overview page * Home * Job Overview Career HTL\-Absolvent Elektrotechnik / Mechatronik (w/m/d) \- Montageelektrik Kranmontage 22\.05\.2026 Seit mehr als 90 Jahren setzt PALFINGER weltweit Maßstäbe mit innovativen Kran\- und Hebelösungen. Als eines der führenden Technologie\- und Maschinenbauunternehmen verwandeln wir die Bedürfnisse unserer Kunden in nahtlos integrierte Lösungen und schaffen gleichzeitig Möglichkeiten für unsere Mitarbeitenden, zu wachsen, zusammenzuarbeiten und Grenzen zu überschreiten. Bei PALFINGER glauben wir an die Kraft des Miteinanders: indem wir uns gegenseitig stärken, Innovationen vorantreiben und gemeinsam Ideen zum Leben erwecken. Werde Teil unseres Teams. B. in Mechatronik, Elektrotechnik oder Automatisierungstechnik * Interesse an Technik, Elektrik und Maschinen sowie Motivation, Neues zu lernen * Praktisches Verständnis und eine sorgfältige, selbstständige Arbeitsweise * Bereitschaft zum 2‑Schichtbetrieb * Gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift Erste Erfahrung ist von Vorteil, aber kein Muss – entscheidend sind Motivation und Lernbereitschaft. WAS WIR BIETEN * Attraktive Entlohnung inklusive Prämienmodell und etwaige Schichtzulagen * Betriebsrestaurant mit frisch zubereiteten Gerichten und einem täglichen Essenszuschuss * Betriebliches Gesundheitsmanagement "PALfit" mit Aktionen, Sportangeboten und Bikeleasing * Jobticket für den Weg von und zu PALFINGER / kostenloser Parkplatz * Zahlreiche Mitarbeitervergünstigungen und \-angebote Wir bieten ein attraktives und leistungsbezogenes Gehalt, das sich an der Qualifikation und Berufserfahrung orientiert und das für diese Position deutlich über dem Kollektivvertrag liegen kann (KV\-Minimum auf Basis einer Vollzeitbeschäftigung ist **EUR 47\.546,94** brutto pro Jahr). Dich interessiert der Job und du hast noch Fragen? Kontaktiere uns gerne. title: Description: Internship AI & LLM | Center of Excellence Digital Manufacturing (f/m/d)
description: * Job Overview * Back to Career Overview page * Home * Job Overview Career Internship AI \& LLM \| Center of Excellence Digital Manufacturing (f/m/d) 02\.06\.2026 For more than 90 years, PALFINGER has set benchmarks with innovative crane and lifting solutions worldwide. As a leading technology and mechanical engineering company, we transform customer needs into seamlessly integrated solutions while providing opportunities for our people to grow, collaborate, and push beyond limits. We foster an environment where we reach up, reach out, and reach beyond. Become part of our team and let’s lift each other to create what’s next. To strengthen our Center of Excellence Digital Manufacturing team in **Lengau** , we are looking for a **working student** with a strong interest in **AI / Large Language Models** to support us in the **second half of 2026** . In this internship you will develop an internal chatbot for Global Operations Excellence—connecting existing knowledge (SharePoint, Azure DevOps) with production data (SQL) to support day\-to\-day decisions and continuous improvement. Sounds like a good fit? Then we look forward to receiving your application. START / DURATION September–December 2026 (12–16 weeks) **Your Responsibilities** * Connect and curate knowledge sources (central SharePoint, Azure DevOps and similar systems) for chatbot use * Design and build an LLM\-based chatbot that answers questions grounded in the available documents (e.g., standard supplier recommendations for new equipment) * Collaborate with ICT to integrate the solution into the global AI platform and governance structure * Enrich the chatbot with operational data (production KPIs, machine signals) via scheduled daily queries from multiple SQL databases (no real\-time requirement) * Implement site\-based access control and prototype root\-cause analysis / improvement recommendations **Your Qualifications** * Near completion of a bachelor’s degree (or ongoing master’s) in AI, Data Science, Computer Science, Industrial Engineering, or a comparable field * Solid programming skills and experience with data handling; SQL knowledge is a plus * First experience with LLMs, prompt engineering, and/or RAG concepts (e.g., embeddings, vector search) is an advantage * Ability to work independently, take ownership, and deliver results * Creative, out\-of\-the\-box thinking with a hands\-on, solution\-oriented mindset * Strong communication skills and enjoyment of working in cross\-functional teams (operations \& ICT) * Very good English skills (spoken and written) **What We Offer** * Flexible working hours with no core time and possibility to work from home on a regular basis * Further training and international career opportunities * Corporate health management "PALfit" * Company restaurant with freshly prepared meals and daily meal subsidy * Job ticket for travel to and from PALFINGER * Numerous employee discounts through Corporate Benefits For this position, considering the collective agreement for the metal industry, a gross monthly salary of **EUR 1,949\.22** is paid on a full\-time basis. Are you interested in the job and still have questions? Feel free to contact us. Quick application Apply with registration Andrea Ostermann Talent Acquisition Specialist email jobs@palfinger.com Location Lengau Palfinger EMEA GmbH Kapellenstraße 18 , 5211 Friedburg Austria Palfinger Lengau Location benefits * Learning Opportunities * Company Doctor * Food Allowances * Company mobile phone (function related) * Fitness Programs * Flexible Working Hours * Health Measures * Good transport connection * Homeoffice (function related) * Canteen * Employee discounts * Parking facilities * Job Ticket

title: DevOps Engineer
description: We are partnering with a leading insurance client on a large\-scale transformation programme and are seeking a DevOps / QA Engineer to support the delivery of resilient CI/CD infrastructure, automated testing, and deployment reliability across critical enterprise platforms. This role sits between DevOps, platform engineering, and quality assurance teams, with a strong focus on automation, platform resilience, and continuous improvement. While not a formal management role, this position will suit someone who enjoys taking ownership, driving standards, and acting as a technical lead across CI/CD and QA initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Build, optimise, and maintain robust CI/CD infrastructure and deployment pipelines * Develop and maintain automated smoke, integration, performance, and security testing within the deployment lifecycle * Design and implement automated rollback and failover procedures to minimise downtime and improve platform resilience * Partner closely with engineering teams to identify build failures and drive technical remediation activities * Support continuous improvement across deployment automation, testing standards, and release quality * Prepare and deliver weekly system demonstrations covering infrastructure health, performance benchmarks, and security scanning metrics * Contribute to best practices across DevOps, QA automation, and release engineering processes **Required Experience** * 4\+ years’ experience across DevOps, CI/CD engineering, and QA/testing environments * Strong experience with CI/CD pipelines, deployment automation, and version control workflows * Hands\-on experience with automated testing frameworks for integration and infrastructure testing * Strong scripting capability with experience writing reusable automation and verification scripts * Experience troubleshooting deployment failures and supporting remediation activities across engineering teams * Strong communication skills with the ability to work cross\-functionally across technical teams **Desirable Experience** * Experience within enterprise or regulated environments * Exposure to cloud infrastructure and Infrastructure as Code practices * Experience supporting highly available production systems **How to Apply** If you are a DevOps / QA Engineer looking for a role with strong technical ownership please apply with your latest CV.

title: Digital Marketing - Acquisition - Dutch Speaker
description: Due to continued expansion and growth, Accenture is currently recruiting for a Partner Manager to drive the rollout of a unique digital marketing advertising solution to EU markets. We are partnering with Pinterest to ensure businesses are set up for success on the platform. You will support new clients from the very start of their Pinterest journey and help to educate, inspire and help advertisers define the best type of campaigns to drive results and achieve their goals. This is an opportunity to work with one of the fastest\-growing players in social media, with the support of a leading global professional services company. Our high\-performing Pinterest team has a proud track record. We maintain a positive, high\-performing team environment by looking out for each other, cherishing diversity and taking time to stop and celebrate success together regularly. **Responsibilities** * We put the power in your hands; to identify, research and contact potential new partner businesses that you believe Pinterest’s unique business proposition could bring value to, delivering personalised product pitches. * Setting the advertiser up for success before passing over to a colleague in Account management to be scaled * You will also work with businesses and brands that have already started advertising on the platform to understand their strategic and tactical goals and co\-create the right marketing plan to achieve them. * Gain an in\-depth understanding of partners' business goals in their respective markets, offering educated insights to help them to succeed. * Developing and growing long term relationships as the ‘face’ \& first point of contact for your clients. * Work toward fixed KPIs \& a quarterly sales target. Work collaboratively with the wider sales team in order to share (and gain) valuable knowledge on your * . \#LI\-EU **Basic Qualifications** * Completed High School Diploma (Irish Leaving cert or equivalent) **Qualifications** * At least one year of experience in a target\-driven, customer\-facing role (sales is preferred). * In\-depth, demonstrated knowledge of digital marketing, including understanding of metrics such as CPA, CPC, CPM and ROAS. * Demonstrated experience using analytical approaches to solving business problems. * Demonstrable ability to train and educate advertisers on solutions including how to create and optimise campaigns. * Demonstrable ability to understand, validate, and overcome customer objections by crafting a compelling marketing plan to use Pinterest’s platform \& functionality to achieve advertisers goals. * Ability to learn quickly and maintain current, comprehensive knowledge of the client’s products and services. * Tech\-savvy with an affinity for innovative and emerging technology. * Self\-reliant, adaptable, decisive, and professional; able to effectively multi\-task in a dynamic environment. * Proven experience in working toward quarterly goals, in a KPI\-driven environment. **What do we offer:** * Hybrid role (3 days working from Office and 2 days working from home) * Monday to Friday * Health Insurance * Social team events (indoor and outdoor) * 20 days annual leave \+ 2 Company days \+ 2 volunteers. * Recognition points which you can spend on shopping or vouchers. *Please also note that Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, or gender identity.* *Please advise if you have any particular requirements, so we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at* *accessIErecruitment@accenture.com*

title: Double-Manned HGV Driver Teams
description: **Job Overview:** We are currently seeking experienced and reliable **Double\-Manned HGV Driver Teams** to join our transport operation based at our Dublin depot. This role involves long\-distance deliveries and collections throughout **Ireland, the UK, and mainland Europe**, working collaboratively as a two\-driver crew to ensure goods are transported safely, efficiently, and on schedule. The successful team will be responsible for maintaining delivery schedules, complying with all road transport regulations, and providing a professional service to our customers across multiple jurisdictions. Strong communication skills, excellent teamwork, and a commitment to the highest standards of safety and compliance are essential. Both drivers must hold valid HGV licences, possess up\-to\-date Driver CPC certification, and have a good understanding of Irish, UK, and European road networks and transport regulations. Experience in international transport operations, including ferry crossings and European route planning, would be highly advantageous. This is an excellent opportunity for professional driving teams seeking regular long\-haul work, competitive rates, and the opportunity to operate across Ireland, the UK, and Europe as part of a well\-established and growing transport operation. **Key Responsibilities:** * Carry out scheduled deliveries across Ireland during night hours. * Ensure timely and accurate delivery of goods to customer locations. * Complete all necessary paperwork, including delivery dockets and timesheets. * Carry out pre\- and post\-trip vehicle checks and report any issues. * Maintain a clean and professional appearance of both yourself and your vehicle. * Adhere to all driving regulations, company procedures, and road safety laws. * Communicate effectively with the Transport Office regarding delays, route issues, or delivery updates. * Ensure secure loading and unloading of goods, maintaining load safety throughout the journey. * Represent the company in a professional and courteous manner at all times. **Requirements:** * Full clean **Category C\+E (Artic) Licence**. * Valid **CPC card** and **Digital Tachograph card**. * Strong knowledge of Irish roads and delivery routes. * Good communication and time management skills. * Ability to work night shifts and flexible hours when required. * Reliability and a strong safety\-first mindset. **Desirable:** * Experience with refrigerated or multi\-drop deliveries. * Previous experience delivering to RDCs or retail depots. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: From €40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * On\-site parking Application question(s): * CPCs up to date ? Experience: * HGV Driving in Ireland: 2 years (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Drawings Room Host
description: The Merrion, one of Dublin's finest luxury hotels, is recruiting for a **Drawings Room Host.** Centrally located in Dublin within easy reach of public transport, this role will offer you the opportunity to work in a beautiful hotel where the highest of standards are in place and where you have all the tools you need to do your job. Working alongside an experienced team, you will be offered plenty of training. This is a really rewarding role on a team where you can be assured of a warm welcome! **The Role** We are looking for a service driven individual to join our five star Team. This is a fantastic opportunity to either begin or continue your career in one of the Leading Hotels of the World. Working with a professional and welcoming Team, you will be part of creating Merrion Moments for our guests and colleagues with your main duties including: Welcoming and sitting the guest to their allocated tables Controlling the flow of people coming through the Drawing Rooms Directing the guest to other departments if necessary Ensuring that a five\-star, warm welcome is provided to all guests Assisting with service where required **Who We Are Looking For** The successful candidate will have strong guest focus, communication skills, great spoken and written English, multi\-tasking ability and enjoy contributing in a positive way to The Merrion Culture. It is these qualities that require your commitment and will set us apart at being a most caring and successful hotel. Flexibility in response to guest and business needs is required, no two days are the same! *Shift work \& flexibility are required in this position.* Our belief is that the success of The Merrion Hotel depends on the commitment of each member of our Team. The hospitality \& service we provide to our guests must be the very best at all times, and in recognition of this, you will enjoy our excellent benefits which include: * Competitive pay rates * Free hot meals on duty * Free cleaning of company provided uniforms * Birthday gifts and Service Awards * Subsidized taxi travel: we contribute up to €20 of your taxi fare for travel to or from work outside usual public transport times * Travel and Bike to Work \- Tax saving Schemes, discounted parking in the city * Safe scooter and bike parking facilities * Health and Dental Insurance payments * Contributory Pension Plan from the day you start work, including Death in Service benefit * Increased holiday entitlement with length of service * Recommend a Friend scheme: bonus paid to you for recruiting a friend or colleague to join The Merrion team * Employee Discounts for room nights, our Garden Room Restaurant and Cellar Bar for you and your family/ friends * Free Employee Assistance Programme \- free professional counselling and support on everything from anxiety to parenting to exam stress \- for you and all your family members **About The Merrion** The Merrion, a member of the prestigious Leading Hotels of the World, is located opposite Government Buildings in the heart of the city centre. Created from 4 Georgian Townhouses, the 142 bedroom and suites are arranged around two 18th Century style gardens. If you feel this is the role and Company for you, please apply online with your CV \& cover letter detailing what you can bring to us at The Merrion.

title: Electrical Engineer
description: Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions. We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including: Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Key Responsibilities of the role:** * Management of the Electrical Systems under your control. * To support the engineering requirements through managing \& liaising with Key Project Stakeholders (Client design team, Main contractor, Suppliers, Sub\-contractors, etc). * Familiarisation of Design/IFC specifications and drawings to fully understand the scope of the Project works. Overseeing the Co\-ordination process to transform design information into full installation drawings by BIM personnel ensuring spec compliance and as per schedule priority. This will require generation/management of Requests for Information (RFI), Technical Queries etc. * Management of Site Safety in conjunction with the Project Manager and the Mercury Safety Advisor. This will include the setup, management of and closing out of the safety file as part of the Management team. * Management of Site QA in conjunction with Mercury QA Manager including set up, management and close out of file * Management of Project Schedule in conjunction with Mercury Project Manager, consulting Engineer and Client requirements and ensuring Engineering activities do not impact on same (e.g. Procurement etc..). * Management of all aspects associated with your package including: + Mercury Electrical Team + Sub\-contractors + Material deliveries and goods receipting. + Plant and Equipment + Variations + Valuations * Management of all day\-to\-day site operations. * Ensure Mercury Policies and procedures are followed closely. * Management of sub\-contractor activities including tracking progress against schedule and manpower levels. Ensuring adequate information flow to ensure works progress and Weekly Meeting logged. * Ensure Works are installed in accordance with Project specifications and to the Project QA plan (e.g. pre\-agreed benchmarking). * Assisting the Installation Package team to manage the quality needs for the project on site, providing guidance to ensure we are compliant to all relevant standards/codes and specifications associated. This will include all site activities associated with validation and handover to the client. Manage the Construction Work Packs process from development, implementation and handover. This will involve activities onsite to support our construction management team. * Ensure Works are installed in accordance with Project specifications and to the Project QA plan (e.g. pre\-agreed benchmarking). * Management of the Mercury “In Progress” snagging system ensuring that the supervisors return weekly punch lists where applicable. Ensure all snags are distributed to all Sub\-contractors * Issue direct labour hours and sub\-contractor hours to time administrator for tracking centrally. * Attendance at Site Meetings, Internal Project Reviews, Engineering Reviews, Planning and Co\-ordination Meetings and Handover Meetings * Close out of all project deliverables including Final Account, O \& M Manuals, Punch List, Safety File, QA File, Material and Plant re\-locations, Project Plan – all with the support of the site project manager and your project team. * Issue weekly cost reports. This will include all orders placed, materials expected, and labour levels. * Information reporting will be an essential activity to ensure activity targets/deadlines are met to support the project team. There will be an Engineering Task Log generated and managed by the Engineering Lead * Issue internal weekly progress report rolled up from project supervisors report. This will highlight progress percentage complete based on WBS codes and any concerns. It should also highlight any risk elements within the control of Mercury, e.g. late deliveries. * Issue external weekly progress report, to the project manager, highlighting areas not available, delays, shortage of design, third party hold ups and percentage complete. * Management and undertaking of the technical submittal, approvals. * Schedule material deliveries to correspond with the project schedule. Track deliveries. Management of the material procurement utilising Engineering Materials Report (EMR) by producing material take offs identifying the lead in periods and field need rates. * Maintain a weekly project photographic diary. * Preparing Sub\-contract and Supplier Tender Packages in conjunction with Project QS. * Assessment of Sub\-contract and Supplier Tender returns for technical compliance with design intent specifications. * Evaluate and promote the use of new engineering practices and processes to enhance delivery time of the project. * Manage BIM priorities i.e. support team to develop modular concepts, Typical fabrication details and Isometrics generation and to release for construction. Validation of materials/assets within the model are also to be verified for construction. Change management is a critical process to be understood and controlled. * You will be responsible to support and coach all junior engineers assigned to the project team their development is crucial to our organisation and industry. * All other duties associated with the role. **Essential Criteria for the Role:** * 5\+ years’ experience working with a recognised Mechanical/Electrical contractor * Excellent organizational and planning skills * Ability to work as part of a team * Excellent negotiation, problem solving and decision\-making skills * Proficient in use of systems software related to Engineering functions * Excellent IT skills * Familiar with Project /Primavera planning software and CAD/BIM process * Ability to manage workload and prioritise * Excellent communication skills * Ability to work on own initiative **Desirable Skills:** * Knowledge of modularisation (Off\-Site fabrication) * Knowledge of HV \& MV systems * Knowledge of E\&I ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.***

title: Executive Assistance
description: Ireland \- Dublin Product Development and Operations/Full time/Hybrid We are seeking a highly proactive, strategic, and seasoned Executive Assistant to partner with our senior leaders and drive operational excellence across our global teams. Based in our Dublin office, you will serve as the central anchor for leaders and teams spanning Ireland, Poland, and North America (US/Toronto). **Job Description** An Executive Assistant at our company acts as a trusted business partner who anticipates needs, manages complex cross\-border logistics, and supports business objectives for a highly distributed organization. If you are a skilled prioritizer who thrives in a fast\-paced, global environment, we want to hear from you. Location: **In\-Office\- Dublin, Ireland** (Hybrid policy applies: minimum 3 days per week in our Dublin office, with flexibility for occasional remote work). Employment Type: Full\-time What you’ll do * **Strategic Calendar \& Time Management:** Manage complex, high\-volume calendars across multiple conflicting time zones (GMT, CET, EST). Prioritize critical meetings, resolve conflicts independently, and safeguard leaders' time for strategic priorities. * **Operational Support \& Coordination** – Act as the primary coordination hub for executives, managing scheduling requests, meeting preparation, d documentation, and cross\-regional alignment with other assistants. * **Global Travel \& Expense Management:** End\-to\-end planning of complex international multi\-leg travel itineraries (particularly between Ireland, Poland, and North America), ensuring alignment with local compliance, tax, and visa requirements. Manage and reconcile expenses efficiently. * **Onsite Meetings, Events, and Team Offsites** – Plan and run onsite meetings, workshops, and offsites end\-to\-end, including logistics and visitor management, to support PDO priorities and create seamless in\-person experiences. * **Confidentiality \& Discretion:** Act as a gatekeeper for highly sensitive, confidential business information, exercising impeccable judgment at all times. * **Admin Community Leadership:** Partner with and mentor junior administrative staff, contributing to the global EA community within the company to best practices and ensure cross\-coverage. What you’ll bring * **Experience:** 5\+ years of administrative support or executive assistant experience, with a proven track record of supporting senior management (VP/Director level) within a fast\-growing global tech or multinational environment. * **Global Matrix Competency:** Strong experience supporting distributed or remote teams across European and North American time zones. You understand how to bridge cultural and logistical gaps. * **Project Management Skills:** Ability to manage multiple activities simultaneously, handle ambiguity with grace, and pivot seamlessly under pressure. * **Communication Mastery:** Exceptional verbal and written communication skills in English; ability to interface confidently with executives and cross\-functional partners worldwide. * **Tech\-Savviness:** Advanced proficiency with Google Workspace (Gmail, Calendar, Docs/Sheets/Slides), Slack, Zoom, and modern project management tools (e.g., Asana, Jira, Mondaydotcom). You leverage technology to automate and streamline work. * **Problem\-Solving Mindset:** A highly autonomous ownership mindset. You don’t just flag issues; you come to the table with potential solutions. Nice to have * Experience supporting Engineering, Product, or highly technical global operations teams. * Familiarity with Polish business culture or basic language skills (though English is our primary business language). * Prior experience navigating complex corporate travel and expense policies across EMEA and AMER. Work hours and travel * **Hours:** Core hours are aligned with the Dublin business day, but this role requires deliberate flexibility to accommodate early/late touchpoints with Poland (CET) and the US/Toronto (EST). * **Travel:** Occasional international travel (approximately 10\-15%) to Poland or North America for key leadership summits or team events. Success looks like * **Operational Synergy:** Leaders' priorities are seamlessly executed with zero friction, and global team alignment feels effortless despite time zone barriers. * **Flawless Execution:** High\-stakes meetings and multi\-regional events are executed on budget, on time, and yield clear, actionable follow\-ups. * **Proactive Influence:** You are recognized as an indispensable business partner who optimizes operations, structures chaotic environments, and builds a cohesive culture across Dublin, Poland, and the US. Interested in this position? **About Guidewire** Guidewire is the platform P\&C insurers trust to engage, innovate, and grow efficiently. We combine digital, core, analytics, and AI to deliver our platform as a cloud service. More than 540\+ insurers in 40 countries, from new ventures to the largest and most complex in the world, run on Guidewire. As a partner to our customers, we continually evolve to enable their success. We are proud of our unparalleled implementation track record with 1600\+ successful projects, supported by the largest R\&D team and partner ecosystem in the industry. Our Marketplace provides hundreds of applications that accelerate integration, localization, and innovation. For more information, please visit www.guidewire.com and follow us on Twitter: @Guidewire\_PandC. Guidewire Software, Inc. is proud to be an equal opportunity and affirmative action employer. We are committed to an inclusive workplace, and believe that a diversity of perspectives, abilities, and cultures is a key to our success. Qualified applicants will receive consideration without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, citizenship, marital status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or disability. All offers are contingent upon passing a criminal history and other background checks where it's applicable to the position.

title: Experienced Garden Designer
description: **Company:** Online Garden Designs **Location:** Ireland (Studio\-based, with client meetings and site visits) Online Garden Designs is a contemporary garden and landscape design studio creating thoughtful, beautifully resolved outdoor spaces for clients across Ireland, the UK and internationally. We combine strong planting design, spatial clarity and ecological awareness with a highly visual, digitally supported design process. Our work spans residential gardens, rural landscapes and bespoke planting schemes, delivered through a studio\-based workflow that blends in\-person collaboration, client\-facing design work and efficient digital production. We are an ambitious and fast\-growing studio. Our recent **Bloom Awards wins** reflect the quality, creativity and planting\-led focus of our work, and we are now looking to strengthen the core team as we move into our next phase of growth. **The Role:** We are looking for an experienced, multi\-skilled Senior Garden Designer to take a leading role in the studio. This is a key position within Online Garden Designs. You will lead projects from concept through to detailed design and delivery, manage client relationships in person and on\-site, and help shape the design standards and workflow of the studio as it grows. You will also play an important role in mentoring junior designers, improving internal systems, and contributing to the long\-term direction of the practice. This is a studio\-based role with regular client meetings, site visits, and collaborative in\-person design development, supported by a strong digital workflow. **What you will do:** \-Lead garden and landscape design projects from concept to completion \-Produce high\-quality design packages including concept plans, planting plans, and construction drawings \-Attend client meetings and site visits, managing relationships confidently and professionally \-Coordinate with contractors, suppliers, and collaborators \-Supervise and/or carry out planting on projects \-Contribute to and refine the studio’s design language, systems and workflow \-Mentor and support junior designers where needed \-Help shape the future growth and structure of the studio **We are looking for someone with:** \-A degree, diploma or equivalent qualification in garden design or landscape architecture \-3\+ years’ professional experience in a design practice \-Strong conceptual and spatial design ability \-Excellent planting design knowledge with a strong ecological sensibility \-Hands\-on horticultural experience, including planting implementation \-Confidence delivering projects through to construction and completion \-Proficiency in Autocad, SketchUp and Adobe Creative Suite \-Strong communication and client\-facing skills \-Ability to manage projects independently from start to finish \-Willingness to travel nationwide for site work and project delivery \-A proactive, ambitious mindset with interest in studio growth and development \-A full driving licence (required) **What we offer:** \-Competitive salary, dependent on experience \-Performance\-related bonus structure \-Studio\-based role with collaborative working environment \-Regular site work and hands\-on involvement in project delivery \-Paid holiday allowance plus bank holidays \-Company computer and software support where required \-Opportunities to work on varied, high\-quality residential and landscape projects \-A central role in a growing, ambitious studio with clear scope for progression **How to apply:** Please send your CV and portfolio, along with a short covering note explaining why you would like to join Online Garden Designs, to: info@onlinegardendesigns.ie

title: Fire Engineer
description: **About the Role** At ORS, we deliver practical, commercially focused solutions that help our clients achieve successful project outcomes. We empower our people with responsibility from day one, while providing the support, expertise, and collaborative environment needed to succeed. We are seeking an experienced Fire Engineer to support the delivery of projects for one of Ireland’s leading retail operators. Working within a fast\-paced environment, the successful candidate will play a key role in ensuring fire safety, regulatory compliance, and practical risk management solutions across operational retail environments. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced Fire Engineer seeking responsibility, variety, and exposure to high\-profile projects within a dynamic and growing organisation. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide fire engineering support throughout design and construction phases. * Review and advise on fire strategies and compliance with Irish Building Regulations. * Identify, assess, and help manage fire\-related risks across projects. * Collaborate with design teams, contractors, and stakeholders to resolve fire safety matters. * Undertake site inspections and project reviews as required. * Develop practical fire safety solutions suitable for live operational environments. **Essential Requirements** * Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in Fire Engineering. * Proven experience in fire engineering within a consultancy, construction, or related environment. * Strong knowledge of Irish Building Regulations and fire safety design principles. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. * The ability to work effectively across multiple projects in a fast\-paced environment. We foster a culture of inclusion for all employees that respects and supports their individual strengths and experiences.

title: Full-Stack Developer - Architect (Azure & Application Modernisation)
description: ### **Description** **Job Specification** *Role Title*: Senior Full\-Stack Architect (Azure \& Application Modernisation) *Location*: Dublin, Ireland or UK (Hybrid) *Department*: Application Modernisation \& Enterprise Integration **About Codec** Codec is an award\-winning digital transformation company with big growth ambitions. We are an established full\-stack IT consultancy, a local alternative to the Big 4, headquartered in Dublin with offices in Galway, Belfast, London, Poland, and Germany. We are growing rapidly and have crafted a clear vision of the future, developing our own ‘IP’ to complement the Cloud stacks from Microsoft and Oracle. As we expand our Application Modernisation team, we are seeking a "Hands\-On Architect"—someone who can design complex systems on a whiteboard and then sit down to write the core code that powers them. **The Role** This is a hybrid **Architect/Developer** role. You will be part of a cross\-functional team delivering robust, user\-friendly public\-facing portals and enterprise integration hubs. You are the technical standard\-bearer for the squad. While the Principal Architect sets the strategy and the Lead runs the project, you own the **Solution Design**. You will decide how the components fit together, define the coding patterns, and mentor the development team to ensure the vision is executed correctly. You will work heavily with the Microsoft UI stack (Razor/Blazor) or Angular 15\+, backed by Azure Serverless technologies. ### **Key Responsibilities** **Solution Design \& Architecture*** **Technical Definition:** Define the granular architecture for web applications, selecting the right Azure services (Web Apps, Functions, Containers) to meet the requirements. * **Integration Strategy:** Design seamless integrations with back\-end systems, specifically focusing on Dynamics 365 Dataverse connectivity and Enterprise Service Bus messaging patterns. * **Database Design:** Architect data layers using Cosmos DB (NoSQL) or SQL Server, ensuring optimal performance for transactional data stores. **Hands\-On Development*** **Core Engineering:** Write the critical path code. You will build the complex REST APIs, background services, and reusable front\-end components that the rest of the team will leverage. * **Full\-Stack Execution:** Work fluidly across the stack, developing responsive portals using Blazor/Razor or Angular, and connecting them to robust .NET C\# back\-ends. **Mentorship \& Quality*** **Technical Oversight:** Act as a technical advisor for a sub\-team of developers. You will review Pull Requests (PRs), enforce coding standards, and provide guidance to reduce technical blockers. * **CI/CD \& DevOps:** Develop and maintain robust CI/CD pipelines and unit test suites to ensure code reliability and smooth deployment to Azure. ### **Skills, Knowledge and Expertise** **Architectural Experience:** 8\-10 years of development experience, with at least 3\+ years acting in a Senior/Architect capacity.* **Microsoft Stack Mastery:** Expert\-level knowledge of .NET C\#, REST API development, and the Microsoft UI Technologies (Razor/Blazor) or Angular 10\+. * **Azure Depth:** Deep familiarity with Azure App Services, Function Apps, and Containerisation. * **Data Fluency:** Proficiency in designing for both NoSQL (Cosmos DB/Mongo) and Relational (SQL) databases. * **Integration Patterns:** Strong working knowledge of Enterprise Service Bus messaging patterns and experience integrating with platforms like Dynamics 365/Dataverse. **Location \& Eligibility** * **Hybrid Working:** Codec supports hybrid working; however, on\-site working will be required where expected by customers. * **Right to Work:** Candidates must be currently located in Ireland or the UK with existing Right to Work approval. ### **About Codec** Established over 40 years ago, Codec is a multi\-award\-winning Microsoft Solutions Partner, trusted by public and private sector organisations to deliver impactful digital transformation. With offices in **Dublin**, **Belfast**, **Cologne**, and across **Germany**, our team of over **300 experts** specialises in harnessing the full potential of Microsoft technologies—including **Dynamics 365**, **Power Platform**, **Azure**, and **Copilot**—to drive innovation, automation, and operational excellence. Codec combines deep industry expertise with a proven track record to help organisations unlock the power of AI and modern cloud solutions Though originally best known as a hardware reseller, since 2010 Codec has focused on building an outstanding software consulting business with a team of experts implementing enterprise solutions using products from Microsoft and Oracle. Our customer base, revenues and average contract values have grown steadily year on year and we continue to invest in the provision of new product and service offerings. Our team is a careful mix of recent graduates and experienced professionals from a variety of diverse cultural backgrounds. We are a melting pot of seasoned consultants supported by the best talent from the local universities. We have a thriving placement student academy and graduate recruitment programme which grows year on year. This company is an equal opportunities employer

title: Gastrointestinal Physiologist ; Chief II
description: **Job Description** **Job Title ;Gastrointestinal Physiologist Chief II** **Department ;Reflux Service** **Reports to ; Head of Endoscopy and Service Line Development** **Date ;2026** **Overall Purpose of Job** To deliver exceptional, patient\-centred care within an environment where quality, respect, caring, and compassion underpin all aspects of service delivery. The Gastrointestinal Physiologist Chief II is responsible for the investigation, interpretation, and reporting of gastrointestinal physiological function to support the diagnosis and management of motility disorders and functional gastrointestinal diseases. The post holder will be a senior and integral member of the Gastrointestinal team, with responsibility for advanced diagnostic testing, staff training and development, quality assurance, audit, and service development in line with hospital strategy and evolving clinical demand. **Key Responsibilities** ### **1\. Diagnostic Testing** Perform, interpret, and report specialised gastrointestinal physiology investigations, including but not limited to: o High\-resolution oesophageal manometry o 24\-hour pH and pH\-impedance monitoring o Small Intestinal Bacterial Overgrowth (SIBO) testing o Hydrogen and methane breath tests Prepare, calibrate, and maintain equipment to ensure accurate and reliable physiological measurements. Monitor patients throughout procedures to ensure safety, comfort, and dignity at all times. ### **2\. Clinical Duties** Support physicians in the diagnosis and management of gastrointestinal motility disorders (e.g. achalasia, gastroparesis). Explain procedures clearly to patients and obtain informed consent in accordance with hospital policy. Develop and maintain patient information leaflets to ensure patients are fully informed prior to investigations. Analyse diagnostic data and produce comprehensive, high\-quality clinical reports. Maintain accurate patient records and ensure compliance with data protection and confidentiality regulations. Actively contribute to innovation, service improvement, and implementation of best practice guidelines and emerging technologies. ### **3\. Education, Training, and Leadership** Provide training, supervision, and mentorship to junior staff, students, and new team members in GI physiology techniques. Support ongoing professional development within the department. Provide patient education regarding test preparation, procedures, and interpretation of results where appropriate. ### **4\. Quality Assurance and Service Development** Participate in audit, quality assurance, and service evaluation activities. Contribute to the development and expansion of GI physiology services in line with hospital objectives. Demonstrate a proactive approach to risk management, quality control, and continuous improvement. **Skills \& Competencies** In\-depth knowledge of gastrointestinal anatomy, physiology, and pathophysiology. Advanced technical expertise in motility and pH monitoring systems. Strong analytical, interpretative, and problem\-solving skills. Excellent communication skills to support effective patient interaction and multidisciplinary collaboration. High level of accuracy and attention to detail in clinical measurement and reporting. **Personal Specification** **Qualifications** * **Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Clinical Measurement Science,** Technological University Dublin (TUD) * **Bachelor of Science (Honours) in Clinical Measurement Science,** Dublin Institute of Technology (DIT) (up to 2018\) or equivalent as confirmed by the IICMP/HSE or * **Certificate in Medical Physics and Physiological Measurement (MPPM)** from Dublin Institute of Technology (*if qualified in or prior to 2005*). * Specialized training in GI physiology (may be gained through accredited courses or on\-the\-job training in a motility lab). **Experience** Minimum of 5 years' post\-qualification experience as a Gastrointestinal Physiologist. Broad and detailed knowledge of gastroenterology diagnostic testing. Demonstrated organisational and time management skills. Evidence of a proactive approach to quality improvement and service development. Management or supervisory experience is desirable. **Job Specific Teamwork Competencies and Knowledge** **Teamwork** Demonstrate ability to work as part of a multi\-disciplinary team. Demonstrate motivation and an innovative approach to job. **Knowledge \& Professional Knowledge** Demonstrate clinical competence and relevant experience. Demonstrate an ability to contribute to overall service development. **Planning \& Organisational Skills** Demonstrate organisational skills including effective time management. Demonstrate the ability to manage deadlines and effectively manage multiple tasks. **Communication \& Interpersonal Skills** Demonstrate effective communication skills including the ability to present information in a clear and concise manner. Demonstrate excellent communication skills especially the ability to communicate clear and concise instructions to patients in an understandable fashion. Demonstrate strong interpersonal skills. **Patient Focus** Demonstrate an awareness and appreciation of the service user. Demonstrate evidence of ability to empathise with and treat patients, relatives and colleagues with dignity and respect. **Personal Competencies** All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager or hospital management. This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the hospital and the post holder develop this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the hospital'

title: General Operative
description: **About Harris Group** Harris Group is Ireland’s leading distributor of commercial vehicles, with a reputation for innovation, reliability, and outstanding customer service. Representing top global brands such as **Maxus**, **Isuzu** and **Higer**, , we operate across Ireland and the UK, supplying vehicles, parts, and aftersales support for a growing network of dealers and customers. **Role Overview** We are currently recruiting for a reliable and hard\-working **General Operative** to join our busy team at our Dublin HQ. The successful candidate will be responsible for the efficient loading and unload of containers within our site as well as general duties, while ensuring a clean, safe, and organised work environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Load and unload 40ft containers as directed by the manager in a safe and efficient way ensuring no damage to stock. * Maintain cleanliness and safety in the work space at all times. * Operate equipment as required. * Support general site activities as directed by the manager. **Key Requirements** * Ability to navigate tight spaces and a high level of driving required. * Attention to detail and accuracy. * Fluent English and good communication skills. * Punctual, dependable, and committed to quality work. **What We Offer** * Competitive hourly rate/salary * Pension scheme * Full training and opportunities for career development * Modern, well\-equipped facilities * Supportive and friendly team environment in a growing company Job Type: Full\-time Work Location: In person

title: General Operative Part Time
description: We are currently seeking a reliable and hardworking General Operative to join our Stoneworks team from now till end of September. This role would suit a school leaver, college student, recent graduate, or anyone available for summer work who enjoys practical, hands\-on work and wants to gain experience in a manufacturing and production environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in the cutting of granite products * Operate machinery and tools used in stoneworks operations (training provided) * Ensure all work is completed to high standards of quality and precision * Follow all health and safety procedures at all times * Maintain a clean, safe, and organised work environment * Work closely with the team to ensure projects are completed on schedule **Requirements** * Good level of English (spoken and understood) * Willingness to learn and take direction * Good work ethic and a proactive attitude * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team * Good level of physical fitness for manual work * Commitment to maintaining a safe working environment Job Types: Part\-time, Temporary Contract length: 3 months Pay: €14\.15\-€15\.00 per hour Work Location: In person

title: Graduate - Service Operations
description: **Company:** Finning (UK) Ltd **Number of Openings:** 1 **Worker Type:** Permanent **Position Overview:** The Finning Service \- Operations Graduate role is designed to build operational, analytical and technical capability and develop future service, engineering and technical leaders within the organisation. The role provides structured exposure to, service delivery, operational processes, continuous improvement and technical problem solving, enabling graduates to develop the knowledge, skills and behaviours required to support safe, reliable and efficient customer operations. Service / technical Graduates will gain experience supporting the end to end service lifecycle, whilst also identifying, analysing and implementing improvement processes, systems and ways of working to drive efficiency and customer outcomes. Depending on business needs, the role may include more technically focused work or more operational / process\-focused work **Job Description:** Major Job Functions: * Learn and apply mechanical, electrical or electronic engineering skills on Caterpillar and associated equipment * Lead or support structured improvement projects focused on efficiency, cost reduction and service performance * Support planned maintenance, inspection and servicing activities under supervision * Assist with fault diagnosis, troubleshooting and repair activities using approved procedures * Use diagnostic tools, systems and technical documentation to support equipment reliability * Analyse service processes and identify opportunities to improve efficiency, quality and customer experience * Support continuous improvement initiatives (e.g. Lean, waste reduction, standardisation, digital process improvements) * Collect and interpret operational data to support decision\-making and performance improvement * Work collaboratively with experienced engineers, supervisors and technical specialists * Maintain accurate service records, reports and documentation * Actively engage in technical training, safety programmes, coaching and performance feedback * Demonstrate Finning values and safety leadership in all workshop and site activities Competencies * Create Safe Environments: Focuses on creating and contributing to a diverse, inclusive, and positive workplace where people are motivated to do their best. * Build Loyalty: Focuses on building strong relationships to deliver common goals. It is about continuously paying attention to customer needs and adapting as these evolve. * Ensure Accountability: Focuses on transparency and reliability, ensuring goals are met by holding self and others accountable for meeting commitments and maintaining high standards. * Prepare and Deliver: Focuses on effective planning, resource management and prioritizing work to meet commitments aligned with the company goals. Specific Skills: * Technical aptitude – ability to learn mechanical, electrical and hydraulic systems * Problem‑solving – structured approach to fault finding and diagnosis * Analytical thinking – ability to interpret data and identify trends/opportunities * Process improvement mindset – identifying inefficiencies and suggesting practical solutions * Commercial awareness – understanding impact of service performance on cost and customer outcomes * Manual and practical skills – working safely with tools, equipment and components * Attention to detail – following procedures and technical standards accurately, as well as identifying potential for efficiency through inventions and process/control updates * Communication – explaining technical issues clearly to supervisors and team members * Teamwork – working effectively within workshop and field\-based teams * Safety awareness – understanding and applying safe systems of work Knowledge: * Foundational understanding of mechanical, electrical or electronic engineering principles * Awareness of preventative maintenance and inspection processes * Understanding of fault diagnosis principles and root cause analysis (developed during role) * Awareness of service operations processes and performance metrics (e.g. utilisation, turnaround time, cost efficiency) * Basic understanding of continuous improvement methodologies (Lean, problem\-solving frameworks, etc.) * Awareness of the importance of equipment reliability, uptime and safety for customers * Understanding of how service quality impacts customer satisfaction and operational performance * Ability to work through problems systematically and propose and implement changes that improve efficiency and/or customer experience Accountability: * Supporting planned maintenance and service activities in line with schedules and procedures * Ensuring work is carried out safely, complying with Finning and site safety standards * Identifying and escalating opportunities to improve processes, efficiency and customer outcomes * Contributing to continuous improvement initiatives within the Service function * Accurate completion of job cards, inspection reports and service documentation * Escalating issues, faults or risks appropriately to supervisors * Maintaining a professional approach when interacting with colleagues and customers * Supporting projects that enhance customer experience Education \& Experience: Completed undergraduate degree (Level 6 or equivalent) in a relevant technical discipline such as: * Mechanical Engineering * Electrical or Electronic Engineering * Mechatronics * Automotive, Plant, Marine or Heavy Equipment Engineering * Engineering Technology or similar technical field Or an equivalent qualification that provides strong engineering fundamentals aligned to service operations. **Experience** * No prior full‑time professional service engineering experience required. * Experience gained through academic study, industrial placements, workshops, apprenticeships, part‑time or voluntary roles is sufficient where it demonstrates one or more of the following: + Exposure to hands‑on mechanical or technical work + Application of safe working practices + Use of tools, equipment or diagnostic systems + Ability to follow procedures and technical instructions + Understanding of operational discipline and teamwork If you would like to be a part of an industry leading company offering career development then apply online today At Finning, we prioritize creating a diverse and inclusive environment. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and we actively encourage all individuals to express themselves and achieve their full potential. As a company, we continuously strive to enhance our outreach to individuals of all backgrounds and identities. We do not discriminate against applicants based on gender identity, race, national and ethnic origin, religion, age, sexual orientation, marital and family status, and/or mental or physical disabilities. Furthermore, Finning is committed to collaborating with and providing reasonable accommodations /adjustments to individuals with disabilities. If you require an adjustment/accommodation at any point during the recruitment process, please inform your recruiter.
